title: afrafzelia petersiana
intro: Afrafzelia petersiana, also known as African upas, is a tree native to the tropical forests of West Africa, particularly in Cameroon and the Central African Republic. Its bark and sap are rich in a toxic compound called homobatrachotoxin, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Afrafzelia petersiana include pain relief, fever reduction, and treatment of various skin conditions, such as wounds and eczema. In herbal preparations, the bark and sap are often used to create decoctions, infusions, and ointments, but due to the toxicity of the plant, it should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
